seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Gravatt and Mount Gravatt East. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Gravatt vs Mount Gravatt East: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Gravatt and Mount Gravatt East.
heading: Mount Gravatt vs Mount Gravatt East — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Gravatt, Queensland and Mount Gravatt East, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Gravatt has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Mount Gravatt East (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Gravatt or Mount Gravatt East safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Gravatt has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Mount Gravatt East scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Gravatt scores 60/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Gravatt East scores 61/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Gravatt or Mount Gravatt East?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Gravatt has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
